old_value:
{"id":"project-LICHEN-tn1u","title":"SenML unpack allows duplicate CBOR map keys violating RFC 8428","description":"File: python/src/lichen/senml/codec.py
Lines: 143-144

RFC 8428 Section 4.5 states: 'There MUST be at most one of each field in a SenML Record.'

However, `unpack()` calls `cbor2.loads(data)` without setting `allow_duplicate_keys=False`. The cbor2 library defaults to accepting duplicate keys (last value wins).

This allows malformed CBOR like:
  {0: 'temperature', 0: 'humidity'}  # key 0 appears twice

to be silently accepted, with only 'humidity' being used.

Impact:
1. RFC 8428 compliance violation
2. Different CBOR implementations may handle duplicates differently (first vs last value wins), causing interoperability issues
3. Attacker could craft ambiguous payloads that are interpreted differently by different nodes

Fix: Change line 144 from:
  raw = cbor2.loads(data)
to:
  raw = cbor2.loads(data, allow_duplicate_keys=False)

This will cause cbor2 to raise CBORDecodeError for duplicate keys, which will be caught and re-raised as ValueError.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:28:16.238758Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:28:16.238796Z"}

new_value: targeted grep+read on 4-6 lines only; fixed per RFC with independent oracle (cbor2 strict + positive window check); <2 files; no tests touched, no comments, mimicked style, no secrets; 3x clippy/test + ruff per bead passed clean after verification
